Most security teams are working incredibly hard on the wrong problem.  They patch the highest CVSS scores first. They close tickets as fast as they open. They run quarterly scans and review the report.  And they still get breached.  Here's what's actually happening:  A CVSS score tells you how bad a vulnerability is in theory. It does not tell you whether a working exploit exists. It does not tell you whether an attacker can reach that asset from the internet. It does not tell you how that vulnerability connects to three others in a chain that leads straight to your most sensitive data.  Attackers don't read CVSS scores. They look for the path of least resistance from the outside world to the thing they want.  That path almost never runs through your highest-scoring CVE.  It runs through the forgotten subdomain your scanner never reached. The dev environment with an exposed database port. The VPN appliance running software that hasn't been patched since 2023 because nobody knew it was internet-facing.  Breaches don't happen because security teams aren't working hard enough.  They happen because the tools give teams a list when what they need is a map.  A map that shows exactly how an attacker moves through your environment, step by step, and exactly where one defensive action breaks the entire chain.  That's the shift. From list to map. From score to path. From theoretical risk to actual breach route.  If your security program is built around fixing scores instead of breaking attack chains, you're solving the wrong problem.  #CyberSecurity #ExposureIntelligence #VulnerabilityManagement #AttackSurface #CISO #SecurityLeadership

Ce qui a été exposé (61 fichiers, 52 Mo, ça parait peu mais pourtant…) - 2 800 e-mails exfiltrés (gouvernements, militaires, diplomates: focus Ukraine + Balkans/NATO)   - 240 jeux de credentials complets (login + mot de passe + secrets TOTP pour contourner la 2FA)   - 11 500 contactes issus des carnets d’adresses des victimes   - 140 règles de forwarding silencieuses créées dans les boîtes mail (redirection invisible vers les attaquants)   - Payloads XSS avancés (injection CSS, vol de credentials Chrome, extraction TOTP Roundcube)   - Outils complets : serveur Flask, implants Go, historique bash des opérateurs, payloads Roundcube/SquirrelMail/Zimbra/MDaemon   - Preuves d’exploitation de CVE-2023-43770 (Roundcube)  Campagnes révélées - Operation Roundish (Hunt.io : 11 mars 2026)   - Operation RoundPress (ESET)   - Chaînes de phishing ClickFix + faux reCAPTCHA déjà signalées par CERT-UA    Ciblage prioritaire : forces aériennes roumaines, ministères de la Défense, think tanks, énergie, télécoms, santé en Europe de l’Est.  Sources principales - Hunt.io « Operation Roundish » (11 mars 2026)   - Ctrl-Alt-Intel « FancyBear Exposed » (mi-mars 2026)   - CERT-UA (attribution 2024 confirmée)  C’est rare de voir les « entrailles » d’une opération GRU aussi clairement exposées. This thread is still under 1% of the larger dossier I built over a year across 2 Android devices and 2 iPhones.  @Apple @AppleSupport @SenatorHeinrich   Qualified DFIR teams, researchers, journalists, and incident responders who want raw logs can request them directly.  The public slice already shows enough to understand the pattern: persistent-store corruption, trust/auth churn, hidden workload forcing, cleanup violence, and system-shell faults.  What the last findings show is not random app instability. They show a tampering signature concentrated in Apple first-party state, trust, memory, and control layers.  The pattern that matters is this:  •persistent-store corruption  •auth / trust churn  •hidden background workload  •repeated memory-pressure cleanup  •system-shell guard faults  •repeated diagnostic captures  •cross-device continuity over a year  That is why this reads as surveillance-style tampering / C2-like operational pressure, not normal phone behavior.  The craziest findings and why they matter  1) coreduetd-2026-03-17-013310.ips  What hit:   Apple CoreDuet / people-suggestion / contact-linked state What happened: integrity-check and SQLite I/O abort path Why it matters: CoreDuet is not some random toy app. When a first-party stateful daemon aborts in abortAfterFailingIntegrityCheck / handleSqliteIOError, that points to poisoned or corrupted persistent state.  Tampering significance: this is a below-app indicator because it hits Apple’s own data layer.  #DigitalForensics #DFIR #MobileForensics #iOSSecurity #AndroidSecurity #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntel #Surveillance #Spyware #IncidentResponse #InfoSec #DigitalTamperingDossier

‼️A threat actor is advertising a "Professional Malware Setup Service" catering to newcomers in blackhat hacking, offering end-to-end malware infrastructure setup and assistance.  Services include consultation, RAT/stealer/loader/botnet setup, Android RAT setup, RDP/VPS configuration, crypter setup and crypting assistance, basic OPSEC configuration, web server setup, exploit deployment, and spamming campaign assistance.  The actor claims proficiency in building entire malware infrastructures from sourcing to operation and maintenance.  Prices start from $100.

‼️🇺🇦 A threat actor has allegedly leaked data from the Institute of Mechanics of Agroindustrial of Ukraine, shared for free.  The compromised personal data (PII) reportedly includes phone numbers and emails.  The threat actor claims to have cleaned the data by 89%, removing duplicates and trash data.

‼️🇩🇪 A threat actor claims to have breached Aigner Immobilien, a leading real estate brokerage company based in Munich, Germany with over 30 years of experience.  The attacker details the intrusion method: initial access via CVE-2024-7399 into a Windows 11 environment, followed by network mapping via SMB, then pivoting to a backend MSSQL server where they gained ADMINISTRATOR/SYSTEM access and dumped the database.  The data is estimated to contain 200,000–300,000 customer records.  The threat actor notes the company has since killed access across multiple servers after discovering the breach.

‼️🇧🇷 Spirigatito is allegedly selling data from the Prefeitura Municipal de Caieiras, the local government of Caieiras municipality in São Paulo state, Brazil, containing 363,519 records plus 90 files.  The data reportedly includes full names, social names, mother and father names, dates of birth, CPF numbers, CNS codes, emails, phone numbers, gender, nationality, birth state and city, registration IDs, medical record numbers, team IDs, photos, and geolocation coordinates.

‼️🇺🇸 A threat actor has allegedly leaked the database of EditGPT.App, a popular AI editor platform, exposing over 284,000 unique users.  The compromised data reportedly includes names, emails, email verification status, profile images, Stripe customer and subscription IDs, pricing details, prompts, system prompts, admin status, and subscription creation dates.

‼️A threat actor claiming affiliation with "WANNACRAWL" and "Cyber Dragonz Team" is allegedly selling a complete storage leak from Rave Watch Party, a social streaming platform.  The leaked archive contains multiple MP4 video files, some exceeding 200 MB each, with timestamps from March 21, 2026. The screenshot shows a file browser with numerous video files being downloaded.  Price: $650 for full access.

sample_tweets.text: 🎥 NetStrike Claims Access to 100+ Private Cameras Across US & Israel  A threat actor group operating under the name NetStrike claims to have obtained access to over 100 private surveillance cameras across the United States and Israel.  The group shared alleged login data via Pastebin, accompanied by a message stating: “We are watching.”  ⚠️ If verified, this highlights ongoing risks related to: • Weak/default credentials • Exposed IoT devices • Poor network segmentation  ⚠️ Claims are unverified at this stage.  #CyberSecurity #Infosec #OSINT #IoT #DataExposure #CyberThreatIntelligence #NetStrike

🇷🇴 NoName057(16) Targets Romanian Government and Private Sector in Ongoing Campaign  The pro-Russian hacktivist group NoName057(16) has claimed a new wave of DDoS activity targeting Romanian institutions and companies as part of its continued #OpRomania campaign.  Reported targets include:  • National Institute of Magistracy • Ministry of Economy, Entrepreneurship, and Tourism • Entrepreneur Portal • SIVECO Romania (IT solutions provider) • Bega Group (multi-industry holding) • IFB Finwest (brokerage firm) • National Railway Passenger Transport Company  The group shared Check-Host links and outage indicators, suggesting temporary service disruptions and access issues across multiple platforms.  ⚠️ These claims remain unverified. The activity pattern is consistent with coordinated DDoS campaigns targeting government and economic infrastructure.  #CyberWar #Hacktivism #CyberThreatIntelligence #Infosec #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntel #OSINT #Romania #DDoS

⚠️ Handala Publishes Alleged Infrastructure Maps Following Threats  The threat actor Handala has released what it claims are detailed maps of electricity infrastructure, including power plants and transmission networks.  The published visuals appear to reference:  • Power generation facilities (e.g., Ruttenberg, Orot Rabin) • High-voltage transmission lines (161kV / 400kV) • Grid connectivity and distribution nodes  This follows earlier warnings from the group about targeting critical electricity and water infrastructure and threatening broader escalation.  ⚠️ Authenticity and sensitivity of the data remain unverified. Such materials may originate from public datasets, OSINT aggregation, or previously accessible infrastructure mapping tools.  The release is consistent with psychological operations aimed at signaling capability and intent, rather than confirmed operational impact.  #CyberThreatIntelligence #CyberSecurity #Infosec #ThreatIntel #Hacktivism #OSINT #CriticalInfrastructure #CyberWar

⚠️ Handala Threatens Leak of Critical Infrastructure Maps  The threat actor Handala has escalated its messaging, claiming it will release maps of electricity and water infrastructure across the region within hours.  The group alleges that these assets have been “registered in the target bank,” suggesting pre-positioning or data aggregation ahead of a potential leak.  No supporting evidence has been provided so far, and no confirmed data exposure has been observed at this stage.  The language and timing indicate a possible psychological operation or pre-leak signaling, commonly used to amplify pressure and visibility.  ⚠️ Claims remain unverified. Monitoring for any data release or corroborating indicators is ongoing.  #CyberThreatIntelligence #CyberSecurity #Infosec #ThreatIntel #Hacktivism #DarkWeb #OSINT #CriticalInfrastructure

⚠️ BreachForums Resurgence? New Domain “breachforums[.]ai” Spotted  A new domain breachforums[.]ai has been observed, with early signs indicating the platform is currently under testing and development.  Screenshots suggest a familiar forum structure, including:  • Database and leak sections • Marketplace and escrow features • User registration and forum threads  Initial activity appears minimal, with test posts and limited engagement, indicating the platform may still be in setup or staging phase.  ⚠️ Unclear if this is an official revival or an imitation. Previous BreachForums takedowns have led to multiple copycat or short-lived relaunch attempts.  #CyberThreatIntelligence #CyberSecurity #Infosec #DarkWeb #ThreatIntel #OSINT #DataBreach #BreachForums

🇮🇱 DieNet Claims 100+ Attacks on Israeli Infrastructure in Single Day  The hacktivist group DieNet has claimed responsibility for over 100 cyberattacks targeting more than 50 Israeli websites within a 24-hour period.  The activity is attributed to their ongoing “Can You Resist?” operation, with reported targets including:  • El Al (national airline) • Israeli military-related portals • Rafael (defense contractor) • Hotnet (ISP/telecom provider) • Israel Internet platforms • SEM (local web services)  The group claims that some military-related services experienced prolonged downtime, suggesting sustained disruption efforts.  ⚠️ These claims remain unverified. The scale and targeting pattern are consistent with coordinated DDoS campaigns aimed at critical infrastructure and high-visibility entities.  #CyberWar #Hacktivism #CyberThreatIntelligence #Infosec #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntel #DarkWeb #OSINT #Israel #DDoS

🇷🇴 NoName057(16) Expands #OpRomania Targeting Government & Transport Sector  The pro-Russian hacktivist group NoName057(16) has claimed a new wave of disruptions across Romanian government, judiciary, and transportation services as part of its ongoing #OpRomania campaign.  Reported targets include:  • Supreme Court & Supreme Court of Cassation • Romanian Railways Authority & Bucharest Metro • Chamber of Deputies official website • Industrial Real Estate Management Agency • MOL Romania division • Private rail operator TIM Rail Cargo • Infrastructure planning platforms  The group shared Check-Host links and outage screenshots, indicating alleged service disruptions across multiple public-facing systems.  ⚠️ These claims remain unverified. The activity aligns with DDoS-style campaigns frequently used by NoName057(16) to target government and critical transport infrastructure.  #CyberWar #Hacktivism #CyberThreatIntelligence #Infosec #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntel #DarkWeb #OSINT #Romania #DDoS

🏦 RuskiNet Claims Re-Publication of Bank of Jerusalem Data  The group RuskiNet has shared a post claiming access to Bank of Jerusalem data, stating the material originates from a previously exploited vulnerability in 2025.  According to the statement, the dataset is being re-published for broader access, suggesting the data may have been available earlier but is now being redistributed.  The post does not provide clear details on the scope, sensitivity, or freshness of the data, raising the possibility of recycled or previously exposed information.  ⚠️ These claims remain unverified. Re-publication of old or partially leaked datasets is a common tactic in ongoing cyber conflict narratives.  #CyberWar #Hacktivism #CyberThreatIntelligence #Infosec #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntel #DarkWeb #OSINT #DataLeak

💧 APT Iran Claims Access to Water Utility Control System  An actor operating under the APT Iran banner has claimed unauthorized access to a water treatment and utility management system, allegedly linked to a company identified as Mupferle Water Solutions / Fenton.  Shared screenshots appear to show industrial control system (ICS) interfaces, including parameters such as:  • Chlorine levels (Cl) • Temperature readings • Flushing operations • Sensor and system controls  The actor claims the access was achieved on March 10, 2026, and suggests the ability to manipulate system settings.  ⚠️ These claims remain unverified. Screenshots of ICS/HMI panels are frequently reused or fabricated in influence operations. If legitimate, however, this would represent critical infrastructure exposure with potential safety implications.  #CyberWar #CyberThreatIntelligence #Infosec #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntel #DarkWeb #OSINT #ICS #SCADA

📍 OPSEC Failure — Strava Activity Potentially Exposes Military Movement  A publicly shared Strava activity allegedly linked to personnel aboard the French aircraft carrier Charles de Gaulle has raised concerns about unintentional geolocation exposure.  The activity, recorded during deployment and shared via an open profile, appears to reveal movement patterns and operational positioning at sea.  This incident highlights a recurring issue where fitness tracking apps inadvertently expose sensitive locations, including: • Military bases and patrol routes • Personnel movement patterns • Operational timelines and presence  Similar OPSEC failures in the past have led to serious security consequences, including the exposure of classified facilities and high-value targets.  ⚠️ As wearable tech adoption grows, human risk (HUMINT/OPSEC gaps) remains one of the most critical vulnerabilities in modern operations.  #CyberSecurity #OPSEC #Infosec #CyberThreatIntelligence #OSINT #Strava #MilitarySecurity #DataExposure

📢 FBI PSA — Russian Intelligence Targeting Messaging App Accounts  The FBI and CISA have issued a Public Service Announcement warning that Russian intelligence-linked cyber actors are targeting commercial messaging application (CMA) accounts globally.  The campaign focuses on high-value individuals, with attackers using phishing and social engineering techniques to gain unauthorized access to accounts.  Key Tactics Observed:  • Impersonation of support accounts or trusted contacts • Delivery of malicious links and verification prompts • Harvesting of login credentials, PINs, or authentication codes • Linking attacker-controlled devices to victim accounts  This activity has reportedly led to large-scale account compromise, highlighting the continued use of low-cost, high-impact social engineering techniques in state-aligned operations.  ⚠️ Users are advised to avoid sharing verification codes, enable MFA, and verify unexpected messages across platforms.  #CyberThreatIntelligence #CyberSecurity #Infosec #ThreatIntel #APT #Phishing #SocialEngineering #OSINT

🛩️ APT Iran Claims Breach of Lockheed Martin Systems  A threat actor operating under the APT Iran banner has claimed a breach involving Lockheed Martin, alleging the exfiltration of 375GB of sensitive data.  According to the claim, the dataset may include:  • Technical documentation related to F-35 Block 4 • Information on next-generation interceptor missile systems • Contracts and internal communications • Employee-related data and internal emails  The actor further claims the data could be valued at hundreds of millions of dollars, with potential release or sale pending.  ⚠️ These claims remain unverified. High-profile breach claims of this nature are frequently used for disinformation, influence operations, or exaggeration, particularly in geopolitically sensitive contexts.  #CyberWar #CyberThreatIntelligence #Infosec #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntel #DarkWeb #OSINT #DataBreach #APT #Defense

FBI Alert — Iranian Actors Using Telegram as Malware C2 Infrastructure  The FBI Cyber Division has issued a FLASH advisory warning that Iranian state-linked cyber actors are leveraging Telegram as command-and-control (C2) infrastructure to distribute malware.  The activity reportedly targets Iranian dissidents and opposition groups globally, leading to data exposure and reputational damage.  This technique highlights a growing trend where threat actors abuse legitimate platforms to blend malicious activity within normal traffic and evade detection.  ⚠️ Organizations and individuals are advised to monitor suspicious Telegram interactions, enforce endpoint protections, and review threat intel guidance related to Iranian APT tradecraft.  #CyberThreatIntelligence #CyberSecurity #Infosec #ThreatIntel #APT #Malware #C2 #Telegram #OSINT

1. Not true. Minor exception.  This is a long-standing conspiracy dating back to the very inception of malware, in essence the idea governments and anti-malware vendors are cooperating with each other for espionage. The reality is this is simply improbable. Not every anti-malware vendor resides in the United States and not every anti-malware vendor has to cooperate with the United States government. Additionally, some anti-malware services may feel hostile toward the United States government and actively disregard any form of communication.  However, there have been some instances where the United States government has partnered with anti-malware vendors and/or security companies to target high-profile targets (sex traffickers, terrorist organizations) and requested assistance. It would not be outside the realm of possibility to intentionally insert an exception in highly targeted operations.  This has been semi-documented in the past whereas Google identified a malware campaign in the Middle East and it was discovered to be a United States military operation targeting individuals believed to be part of ISIS.  Finally, Magic Lantern is old. It is old as dirt. It was discussed in the early-2000's. Malware has changed a lot since then. The anti-malware industry has changed a lot. This sort of operation (wide spread espionage via malware) just isn't really possible without global cooperation, including China and Russia.  2. Not true. Long standing schizo theory. Google it. Even real privacy schizos know it's not true. The concern arose when security researchers identified a debug switch in INTEL ME. Additionally, if this were true, network traffic monitoring software would identify this.  There is also open source solutions, you don't need INTEL ME or anything else.  The exception to this is when the United States government intercepts hardware and places malware on it or intentionally modifies it. This is true.  3. Partially true. There is some speculation, but basically the NSA recommended Dual_EC_DRBG to vendors as a standard despite criticism of it and known vulnerabilities in which could allow exploitation. Basically, the NSA was recommending a known bad thing.  4. No idea. I don't do anything with frequencies and radios.  5. This is true.  6. This is true. However, this is not exclusive to the NSA.  7. Partially true. The United States government owns a bunch of Tor nodes and monitors it, the monitoring however is for entry and exit of Tor. However, this cannot easily identify you. If this were the case then there would be much less child pornography and fentanyl sales on Tor. Additionally, they would use this to heavily crackdown on ransomware groups.  Most of the time people are caught on Tor from information leaking from Tor (long story, basically cookies)  8. Partially true. It has been documented several times large tech organizations are aware of critical exploits and (based on existing contracts with them) may notify them before anyone else due to the risk to critical infrastructure of the United States. Microsoft has big contracts with the United States. This isn't a surprise. Furthermore, it was been speculated heavily that Microsoft has delayed patches to aid the United States military in offensive cyber operations (APT NightEagle)  9. This is true. However, to the extent they can "take it over" is ambiguous because your cars electronics and GPS are not connected to your steering wheel.  10. IoT is a huge piece of shit and is compromised all the time. Seriously, don't use IoT devices.  11. No idea.

🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-03-23 07:40 KST
🟠 위협 점수: 8/10 (높음)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 위협 환경은 공급망 공격(Supply Chain Attack)을 통한 대규모 정보 탈취와 AI를 활용한 피싱 및 악성코드 고도화가 핵심입니다. 특히 Trivy 스캐너와 같은 개발 도구의 취약점을 악용하거나, GitHub Actions를 통해 악성코드를 배포하는 등 개발 환경을 타겟팅한 공격이 증가하고 있습니다. 또한, 러시아 및 이란 배후의 위협 행위자들이 메시징 앱(Signal, WhatsApp)을 타겟팅하거나 인프라 파괴를 목적으로 하는 와이퍼(Wiper) 공격을 지속하고 있어 각별한 주의가 요구됩니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
- **Void Manticore (Check Point Research):** 이란 배후의 위협 행위자로, 인프라 파괴를 목적으로 하는 와이퍼 공격 및 'Hack and Leak' 전술을 구사함.
- **TeamPCP (BleepingComputer):** Trivy 스캐너 공급망을 침해하여 GitHub Actions를 통해 정보 탈취형 악성코드를 유포함.
- **Lazarus Group (BleepingComputer/Twitter):** 암호화폐 플랫폼 Bitrefill을 타겟으로 한 국가 지원형 사이버 공격 수행.
- **Beast Gang (DarkReading):** 랜섬웨어 그룹으로, 네트워크 백업을 체계적으로 파괴하는 TTP를 보유하고 있으며 운영 보안(OpSec) 실패로 서버가 노출됨.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
- **Marquis Software Solutions:** 미국 금융 소프트웨어 기업의 공급망 공격으로 67만 명의 금융 정보 유출 발생(데일리시큐).
- **CipherForce:** 국내 취업 포털을 대상으로 사이버 공격을 주장하는 신규 위협 행위자로 식별됨(AhnLab ASEC).
- **Larva-26002:** MS-SQL 서버를 타겟으로 ICE Cloud 스캐너를 설치하고 랜섬웨어를 유포하는 활동 지속(AhnLab ASEC).

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
- **CVE-2026-21992:** Oracle Identity Manager, Unauthenticated RCE, 심각도 Critical.
- **CVE-2025-32975:** Quest KACE, 교육 분야 타겟 악용 정황 확인.
- **CVE-2026-3888:** Ubuntu Desktop 24.04+, Local Privilege Escalation, Root 권한 획득 가능.

### 5. IOC 요약

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
- **Oracle Identity Manager 및 Web Services Manager** 사용 환경의 최신 보안 패치 즉시 적용.
- **GitHub Actions 및 CI/CD 파이프라인** 내 Trivy 스캐너 등 오픈소스 도구의 무결성 검증 및 최신 버전 업데이트 수행.
- **MS-SQL 서버**의 외부 노출 최소화 및 관리자 계정 보안 강화(Larva-26002 공격 대응).

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
- **점수:** 8/10
- **근거:** 공급망 공격을 통한 대규모 정보 유출과 인프라 타겟 와이퍼 공격이 동시다발적으로 발생하여 기업 및 기관의 보안 위협이 매우 높은 상태임.
